core.execution.harness module

EXPERIMENT HARNESS – Wraps AI execution with energy measurement

Purpose:

This is the CRITICAL synchronization layer between energy measurement (Module 1) and AI execution (Module 3). It ensures perfect alignment between RAPL counters and code execution.

SCIENTIFIC NOTES:
  • Uses Module 1’s 3‑layer architecture correctly:

    Layer 1: RawEnergyMeasurement (from EnergyEngine) Layer 2: BaselineMeasurement (idle power) Layer 3: DerivedEnergyMeasurement (computed metrics for analysis)

  • ALL THREE LAYERS are returned in results for complete transparency

  • Energy measurement wraps ENTIRE execution (not just API calls)

  • Warmup runs discard first execution to eliminate cache/initialization effects

  • Multiple repetitions (n>=30) for statistical power

  • Cool‑down period between linear and agentic ensures fair comparison

  • Network latency tracked separately for cloud models

  • CPU metrics come from Layer 3’s performance counters

Why this exists:

  • Timestamps inside executors can drift from hardware counters

  • Even 50ms misalignment corrupts orchestration tax measurements

  • This harness guarantees perfect synchronization

Requirements:

Req 3.6: Device Handoff Latency – precise timing alignment Req 1.46: High‑frequency sampling – must capture short agent steps Req 3.5: Cool‑down period between runs

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.execution.harness.ExperimentHarness(config_loader)[source]

Bases: object

Wraps AI execution with energy measurement for perfect synchronization.

Critical design:

energy_start() ← RAPL starts recording executor.execute() ← AI code runs energy_stop() ← RAPL stops recording

This ensures hardware counters align exactly with software execution.

For cloud models, network latency is tracked separately to distinguish orchestration overhead from network delays.

run_agentic(executor, task: str, task_id: str = None, is_cloud: bool = True, country_code: str = 'US', run_number: int = 1) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Run agentic executor with synchronized energy measurement.

Same critical alignment as linear: Energy measurement wraps ENTIRE agentic pipeline.

This captures ALL orchestration tax in one synchronized window: - Planning - Tool execution - Synthesis - Inter-step delays

Uses the same 3‑layer architecture and returns ALL THREE LAYERS.

run_comparison(linear_executor, agentic_executor, task: str, task_id: str = None, cool_down: int | None = None, n_repetitions: int = 30, include_warmup: bool = True, country_code: str = 'US', hardware_info: Dict | None = None, save_to_db: bool = False) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Run multiple comparisons with warmup and statistical analysis.

This is the publishable experimental protocol: 1. Warmup run (discarded) to stabilize system 2. N repetitions of (linear + cool‑down + agentic) 3. Statistical analysis of all runs

Returns results with ALL THREE LAYERS for each run.

run_linear(executor, prompt: str, task_id: str = None, is_cloud: bool = True, country_code: str = 'US', run_number: int = 1) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Run linear executor with synchronized energy measurement.

This uses the 3‑layer architecture correctly and returns ALL THREE LAYERS:

  1. RawEnergyMeasurement from EnergyEngine (Layer 1)

  2. Baseline from BaselineManager (Layer 2)

  3. DerivedEnergyMeasurement from EnergyAnalyzer (Layer 3) ← USED FOR METRICS

save_to_database(results: Dict[str, Any], experiment_meta: Dict[str, Any], hardware_info: Dict | None = None) → int | None[source]

Save experiment results to database.

Parameters:

  • results – Results from run_comparison

  • experiment_meta – Experiment metadata

  • hardware_info – Hardware configuration (optional)

Returns:

exp_id if successful, None otherwise

core.execution.harness.get_workflow_order(rep_num)[source]

Return (first, second) workflow order for repetition (1-based).
